title: podman build --secrets not storing secrets
body:
### Issue Description

I can't get secrets to work. I'm trying to follow the documentation as [described here](https://docs.podman.io/en/v5.6.2/markdown/podman-build.1.html#:~:text=secret%3Did%3Dmysecret%20.-,Build%20an%20image%20using%20a%20secret%20stored%20in%20an%20environment%20variable,%24%20podman%20build%20%2D%2Dsecret%3Did%3Dmysecret%2Cenv%3DMYSECRET%20.,-%24%20podman%20build%20%2D%2Dsecret) but am unable to reproduce it. I'm not sure if this is a bug, documentation, or a keyboard-chair interface problem.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```Dockerfile
FROM python:3.10-slim-trixie AS builder
RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret echo $(cat /run/secrets/mysecret)
```

```bash
MYSECRET='qwerty' podman build --secret=id=mysecret,env=MYSECRET . --no-cache
```


### Describe the results you received

```txt
STEP 1/2: FROM python:3.10-slim-trixie AS builder
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret echo $(cat /run/secrets/mysecret)

COMMIT
--> 256d6061a45d
256d6061a45d300be4aa0928acda4c74fd155f6293e017c57746b2bc94b6e29e
```

### Describe the results you expected

```txt
STEP 1/2: FROM python:3.10-slim-trixie AS builder
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret echo $(cat /run/secrets/mysecret)
qwerty
COMMIT
--> 256d6061a45d
256d6061a45d300be4aa0928acda4c74fd155f6293e017c57746b2bc94b6e29e
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

macoOS 26

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: This is almost certainly a `podman remote` thing - the environment variable is being set on the host, not in the container, so the secret is undefined. Question would be, is this a bug - should we be transporting the environment variable to the server - or a documentation issue (we don't really expect this to work, and you'll need to actually make the secret with `podman secret create`.
@baude My inclination would be that this is a documentation problem, and we should be encouraging `podman secret create` for remote users. Agree?
In the case you've got locally `alias docker=podman`, would this behave consistently? Or does the docker CLI do the env var transport?

Hi there! I've been looking into this issue and wanted to share what I've found so far and get some feedback.

I was able to get a file-based approach working:
`podman build --secret=id=mysecret,src=$HOME/mysecret .`
STEP 1/2: FROM python:3.10-slim
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret cat /run/secrets/mysecret
qwerty
COMMIT
--> 809d25f1d8d4

**What doesn't seem to work on macOS (remote mode):**
1. Environment-based: `MYSECRET='qwerty' podman build --secret=id=mysecret,env=MYSECRET .` - appears to produce no output
2. Secret store: `podman secret create mysecret -` then `podman build --secret=id=mysecret .` - gives:Error: creating build executor: could not parse secrets: faccessat /var/home/core/mysecret: no such file or directory. - The build process seems to look for the secret at /var/home/core/mysecret - a path inside the VM
I'm trying to understand the best path forward here:


Is this primarily a **documentation issue** where we should clarify the limitations of remote mode and recommend the `src=$HOME/...` workaround?

Or is **env variable transport** and/or **secret store path translation** from client to remote engine something worth pursuing? I'm not sure how feasible these would be or if they align with how remote mode is intended to work.


Would appreciate any guidance on which direction makes more sense to explore.

The above works fine on macOS in Docker desktop and also in Rancher, so I would argue it would be beneficial for parity's sake. If you're aiming for drop-in compatibility, then it should work similarly.
Does this being fixed imply that https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17382 is fixed?
I think it does. Did not even notice that we had a dupe of this issue.
